revision.text:
'''いか踊り'''とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名物で、[[イカ]]をテーマにした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]誕生。[[1986年]][[レコード]]化され、市民権を確立。今では、函館にて毎年８月上旬に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られるようになる。港まつり[[パレード]]参加者約３万人のうち１万５千人もが群舞する。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ －イカ踊りを踊ろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!]－いか踊りの視聴が可能。
{{event-stub}}

'''いか踊り'''とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名物で、[[イカ]]をテーマにした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]誕生。[[1986年]][[レコード]]化され、市民権を確立。今では、函館にて毎年８月上旬に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られるようになる。港まつり[[パレード]]参加者約３万人のうち１万５千人が群舞する。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ －イカ踊りを踊ろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!]－いか踊りの視聴が可能。
{{event-stub}}

{{event-stub}} [[Category:函館市|いかおとり]] [[Category:イカ|いかおとり]]

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]](昭和56年)有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。[[1986年]]の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月上旬に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、わっしょい函館(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。

{{event-stub}}

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]](昭和56年)有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。従来の函館港おどりとは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、わっしょい函館(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。
== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!] - いか踊りの視聴が可能。
{{event-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いかおとり}} [[Category:北海道の祭り]] [[Category:盆踊り]] [[Category:動物に関する文化]] [[Category:函館市]] [[Category:イカ]] [[Category:1981年設立]]

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]](昭和56年)有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。従来の函館港おどりとは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、わっしょい函館(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。パレードは3部構成で、主に踊られるのが第三部である。また自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!] - いか踊りの視聴が可能。
{{event-stub}}

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]](昭和56年)有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。従来の函館港おどり、いいんでないかいとは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、わっしょい函館(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。パレードは3部構成で、主に踊られるのが第三部である。また自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている。港まつり開催中は[[盆踊り]]の代わりとしても町内会単位で踊られる。
== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!] - いか踊りの視聴が可能。
{{event-stub}}

== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakodate.cci.or.jp/gaikaku/ika1.htm これが函館いか踊りだ!] - いか踊りの視聴が可能。

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 「函館港まつり」項目中の「わっしょい函館」→「ワッショイはこだて」に表記変更
revision.text:
'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。
== 概要 == [[1981年]](昭和56年)有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。従来の函館港おどり、いいんでないかいとは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる函館[[港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、ワッショイはこだて<!-- 公式ホームページwww.hakodate-minatomatsuri.orgでは「ワッショイはこだて」と表記しているため、改めました。 -->(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。パレードは3部構成で、主に踊られるのが第三部である。また自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている。港まつり開催中は[[北海盆踊り|盆踊り]]の代わりとしても町内会単位で踊られる。
== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html 函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！] - あなたのテーマでディープな函館｜函館市公式観光情報サイトはこぶら
{{event-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いかおとり}} [[Category:北海道の祭り]] [[Category:盆踊り]] [[Category:動物に関する文化]] [[Category:函館市]] [[Category:イカ]] [[Category:1981年設立]]

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]。

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]である。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（昭和56年）、有志により誕生した比較的新しい踊りである。従来の『函館港おどり』『いいんでないかい』とは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]（昭和61年）の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる[[函館港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、ワッショイはこだて<!-- 公式ホームページwww.hakodate-minatomatsuri.orgでは「ワッショイはこだて」と表記しているため、改めました。 -->(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。パレードは3部構成で、主に踊られるのが第三部である。また自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている。港まつり開催中は[[北海盆踊り|盆踊り]]の代わりとしても町内会単位で踊られる。また、函館に寄港する[[クルーズ船]]の見送り時には、函館地区クルーズ振興協議会サポーターズ会員によるイカ踊り等のイベントを行っている。
== 関連項目 == *[[北海盆踊り]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html 函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！] - あなたのテーマでディープな函館｜函館市公式観光情報サイトはこぶら *[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html イベント情報 - クルーズ船見送り隊] - サポータークラブ会員・見送り隊大募集｜函館地区クルーズ振興協議会
{{event-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いかおとり}} [[Category:北海道の祭り]] [[Category:盆踊り]] [[Category:イカに関する文化]] [[Category:函館市の文化]] [[Category:1981年開始のイベント]]

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]である。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（昭和56年）に函館港まつり用として函館市民の有志グループ「いか踊り実行委員会」が考案、普及につとめた踊りである<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677-p691</ref>。従来の『函館港おどり』『いいんでないかい』とは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られた。その後[[1986年]]（昭和61年）の[[レコード]]化の影響も経て、徐々に認知度を高めてゆく。現在、函館にて毎年8月1日から5日に行われる[[函館港まつり]]のメインとして踊られている。港まつりの[[パレード]]、ワッショイはこだて<!-- 公式ホームページwww.hakodate-minatomatsuri.orgでは「ワッショイはこだて」と表記しているため、改めました。 -->(旧・一万人パレード)では参加者約3万人のうち1万5千人が群舞する。パレードは3部構成で、主に踊られるのが第三部である。また自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている。港まつり開催中は[[北海盆踊り|盆踊り]]の代わりとしても町内会単位で踊られる。また、函館に寄港する[[クルーズ船]]の見送り時には、函館地区クルーズ振興協議会サポーターズ会員によるいか踊り等のイベントを行っている。 == 交流 == 「[[いか踊り|函館いか踊り]]」への参加 :「青函ツインシティ交流」を結んでいる[[北海道]][[函館市]]の「函館いか踊り」との間で、隔年で「ねぶた」と「いか踊り」の相互派遣が行われている。[[2006年]]は8月7日のねぶた昼間運行の先頭に「いか踊り」が登場し、[[2007年]]は函館に「ねぶた」が派遣された。

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 出典に沿った内容に見直し
revision.text:
'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]である。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）に函館港まつり用として函館市民の有志グループ「いか踊り実行委員会」が考案、普及につとめた踊りである<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677-p691</ref>。『函館港おどり』や『いいんでないかい～函館港唄～』とは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られ、20名の踊り手により初披露された。その後[[1986年]]（昭和61年）の[[レコード]]化や1990年代の[[まちづくり]]構想『[[イカノポリス計画]]（市の魚にイカを制定）』の影響を受け、徐々に認知度を高めていった<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！ 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>。
[[函館港まつり]]の[[パレード]]、ワッショイはこだて<!-- 公式ホームページwww.hakodate-minatomatsuri.orgでは「ワッショイはこだて」と表記しているため、改めました。 -->(旧・一万人パレード、一万人踊りパレード→三万人パレード)の2部の『サマーカーニバル・子供いか踊り』および3部の『函館いか踊り』で踊られ<ref>"開港１６４周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref>、自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている<ref>"開港１６４周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref>。また、函館に寄港する[[クルーズ船]]の見送り時には、函館地区クルーズ振興協議会サポーターズ会員によるいか踊り披露を行っている<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>。
{{Main|函館港まつり#内容}}
== 交流 == 「青函ツインシティ交流」を結んでいる[[北海道]][[函館市]]の「函館いか踊り」との間で、隔年で「ねぶた」と「いか踊り」の相互派遣が行われている。[[2006年]]は8月7日のねぶた昼間運行の先頭に「いか踊り」が登場し、[[2007年]]は函館に「ねぶた」が派遣された。
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[北海盆踊り]] * [[イカ]] * [[函館港]] - [[函館大火]]：函館港まつりの開催のきっかけになった物事 ** [[函館港まつり]] ** [[坂本森一]]：当時の海運業組合長、谷徳太郎の提案をもとに函館港まつりを実行した函館市長 * [[イカノポリス計画]]：[[青函連絡船]]廃止で今後の[[まちづくり]]に課題があったので、[[北海道中小企業家同友会]]函館支部が考案・実行した構想。同じくイカがモチーフになっている。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html 函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！] - あなたのテーマでディープな函館｜函館市公式観光情報サイトはこぶら *[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html イベント情報 - クルーズ船見送り隊] - サポータークラブ会員・見送り隊大募集｜函館地区クルーズ振興協議会
{{event-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いかおとり}} [[Category:北海道の祭り]] [[Category:盆踊り]] [[Category:イカに関する文化]] [[Category:函館市の文化]] [[Category:1981年開始のイベント]] [[Category:コミュニティ]]

'''いか踊り'''（いかおどり）とは、[[北海道]][[函館市]]の名産[[イカ]]をテーマとした[[踊り]]である。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）に[[函館港まつり]]用として函館市民の有志グループ「いか踊り実行委員会」が考案、普及につとめた踊りである<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677-p691</ref>。『函館港おどり』や『いいんでないかい～函館港唄～』とは違い、誰でもすぐ踊れるとのコンセプトで作られ、20名の踊り手により初披露された。その後[[1986年]]（昭和61年）の[[レコード]]化や1990年代の[[まちづくり]]構想『[[イカノポリス計画]]（市の魚にイカを制定）』の影響を受け、徐々に認知度を高めていった<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！ 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>。
函館港まつりの[[パレード]]、ワッショイはこだて<!-- 公式ホームページwww.hakodate-minatomatsuri.orgでは「ワッショイはこだて」と表記しているため、改めました。 -->(旧・一万人パレード、一万人踊りパレード→三万人パレード)の2部の『サマーカーニバル・子供いか踊り』および3部の『函館いか踊り』で踊られ<ref>"開港１６４周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref>、自由参加枠でもいか踊りが採用されている<ref>"開港１６４周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref>。また、函館に寄港する[[クルーズ船]]の見送り時には、函館地区クルーズ振興協議会サポーターズ会員によるいか踊り披露を行っている<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>。
{{Main|函館港まつり#内容}}
== 交流 == 「青函ツインシティ交流」を結んでいる[[北海道]][[函館市]]の「函館いか踊り」との間で、隔年で「ねぶた」と「いか踊り」の相互派遣が行われている。[[2006年]]は8月7日のねぶた昼間運行の先頭に「いか踊り」が登場し、[[2007年]]は函館に「ねぶた」が派遣された。
== 参考文献 == * 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[北海盆踊り]] * [[イカ]] * [[函館港]] - [[函館大火]]：函館港まつりの開催のきっかけになった物事 ** [[函館港まつり]] ** [[坂本森一]]：当時の海運業組合長、谷徳太郎の提案をもとに函館港まつりを実行した函館市長 * [[イカノポリス計画]]：[[青函連絡船]]廃止で今後の[[まちづくり]]に課題があったので、[[北海道中小企業家同友会]]函館支部が考案・実行した構想。同じくイカがモチーフになっている。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ 函館港まつり] *[http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ はこだてキッズ - イカ踊りをおどろう！] *[http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html 函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう！] - あなたのテーマでディープな函館｜函館市公式観光情報サイトはこぶら *[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html イベント情報 - クルーズ船見送り隊] - サポータークラブ会員・見送り隊大募集｜函館地区クルーズ振興協議会
{{event-stub}}

== 関連項目 == * [[イカ]] * [[函館港]] - [[函館大火]]：函館港まつりの開催のきっかけになった災害 ** [[函館港まつり]] ** [[坂本森一]]：当時の海運業組合長、谷徳太郎の提案をもとに函館港まつりを実行した函館市長 * 披露される場所 ** [[十字街 (函館市)|十字街]] - [[大門 (函館市)|大門]] - [[五稜郭地区]]：繁華街 ** [[若松ふ頭]] - 踊りが披露されるクルーズ船岸壁がある埠頭。旧・[[青函連絡船]]桟橋 * [[イカノポリス計画]]：[[青函連絡船]]廃止で今後の[[まちづくり]]に課題があったので、[[北海道中小企業家同友会]]函館支部が考案・実行した構想。同じくイカがモチーフになっている * [[北海盆踊り]] - 港まつり関連行事で町内会単位で開催される踊り行事は北海盆踊り風である

These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦}}'s seventh month are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon D..."

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
== References == * 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. - {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} - {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: - {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. - {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ** {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ** {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ** {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ** {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ** {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ** {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ** {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ** {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ** {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ** {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ** {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ** {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ** {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ** {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ** {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ** {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.

== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. ''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} ''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: ''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. ''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
