'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Aqqitionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Aqqitionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Aqqitionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Aqqitionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

{{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]]. * {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:兵庫県]] ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

revision.text:
'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002年 [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

Dance-->[[ja:日本三大川祭]]. * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:京都府]] ''' {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:祇園祭]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|en}}<!--Squid {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:大阪三大夏祭り]]. * {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->[[ja:兵庫県]] ''' {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|lt=Yumura Onsen Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023年</ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024年2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988年7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]]

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]]== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]]== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year [[Category:Completion tier 3]]== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> ''' {{ill|Tottori Shan-Shan Festival|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> * {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> ''' {{ill|Awa Odori|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> – Its {{ill|Tokushima City Awa Odori|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> is one of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Shikoku|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->. * {{ill|Kochi Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> ''' {{ill|Yosakoi Festival|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> – One of the Three Great Festivals of Shikoku. * {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> ''' {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> * {{ill|Kumamoto * {{ill|Sabantuy|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> – A summer festival held along the middlereaches of the {{ill|Volga River|en}}<!--Squid Dance--> in {{ill|Russia|en}}<!--Squid Dance-->.

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=draft}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=draft}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=draft}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=draft}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=draft}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港|12=draft}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火|12=draft}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=draft}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=draft}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=draft}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=draft}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=draft}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=draft}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=draft}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from {{ill|Hakodate|ja|函館市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft}}, inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=draft}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=draft}} or {{ill|"Iin de Naikai" - Hakodate Port Song|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=draft}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=draft}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港|12=draft}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火|12=draft}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=draft}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=draft}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=draft}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=draft}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=draft}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=draft}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and {{ill|Aomori|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=draft}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Squid|ja|イカ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hakodate Port|ja|函館港|12=draft}} – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the {{ill|Great Fire of Hakodate|ja|函館大火|12=draft}}. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=draft}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=draft}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=draft}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=draft}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=draft}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=draft}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=draft}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=draft}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|qq=|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|qq=|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
