<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|qq=|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|qq=|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}

Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
{{Event-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:いか踊り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11259055]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ika Odori''' (いか踊り) is a dance from [[:en:Hakodate|Hakodate]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], inspired by the city’s signature product: squid.
== Overview == The dance was created in 1981 by the “Ika Odori Executive Committee,” a volunteer group of Hakodate citizens, for the {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}}.<ref>函館市史通説編4 p677 - p691</ref> Unlike the {{ill|Hakodate Port Dance|ja|函館港おどり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja|いいんでないかい〜函館港唄〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isn't it good? ~Hakodate Port Song~|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the concept of Ika Odori was designed to be simple so anyone could participate. Toshiyuki Masuno, an employee at a fishery company, choreographed the dance,<ref name="212club">マルミ屋宮下食品課長代理増野敏幸 函館 - 212CLUB 創刊2号（弐壱弐研究所 1988th year 7月）102頁</ref> which debuted with a group of 20 dancers.
The dance gained recognition through its recording in 1986 and through the 1990s {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}, a community development initiative that designated squid as Hakodate’s official fish.<ref>函館港まつりで「いか踊り」を踊ろう! 函館観光公式ガイドはこぶら 函館市 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref>
Ika Odori is a prominent feature in Hakodate Port Festival events, such as the “Wasshoi Hakodate” parade (formerly the “10,000-person parade” and later the “30,000-person parade”).<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつりワッショイはこだて開催要綱" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> It is also performed by open participation groups.<ref>"開港164周年記念函館港まつり 「ワッショイはこだて」いか踊りの部 自由参加の参加方法について" 函館港まつり実行委員会 2023rd year </ref> Additionally, the dance is often presented by members of the Hakodate Cruise Promotion Association to send off cruise ships at Hakodate’s port.<ref>[https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/bunyabetsu/kaiun/cruise/cruise3_hakodate2.html "函館地区クルーズ振興協議会の活動"] 国土交通省 2024th year 2月10日閲覧</ref> {{Main|Hakodate Port Festival#Content}}
== Cultural Exchange == As part of the “Seikan Twin City Exchange” between Hakodate and [[:en:Aomori|Aomori]], reciprocal performances of {{ill|Nebuta|ja|ねぶた|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nebuta|1=nebuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q861941}} and Ika Odori are held in alternating years. In 2006, Ika Odori was featured at the opening of the Nebuta daytime parade on August 7, while in 2007, Aomori’s Nebuta float traveled to Hakodate for an exchange.
* 函館市史 通説編第4巻 函館市史編さん室編 函館市 2002nd year == Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Decapodiformes|Squid]] * [[:en:Port of Hakodate|Hakodate Port]] – Related to the Hakodate Port Festival and the aftermath of the [[:en:Great Fire of Hakodate|Great Fire of Hakodate]]. **''' {{ill|Hakodate Port Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} **''' {{ill|Sakamoto Moriichi|ja|坂本森一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Moriichi|1=Shin'ichi Sakamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991760}}, Hakodate mayor who initiated the port festival based on a proposal by maritime union leader Tanitokutaro. * Performance Locations: **''' {{ill|Jūjigai|ja|十字街 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūjigai|1=Jūjigai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118181437}} – {{ill|Daimon|ja|大門 (函館市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimon|1=Daimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439819}} – {{ill|Goryōkaku Area|ja|五稜郭地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōkaku Area|qq=}}, Hakodate’s entertainment district. **''' {{ill|Wakamatsu Wharf|ja|若松ふ頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Wharf|1=Wakamatsu Wharf|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131932261}}, the cruise terminal and former ferry dock. * {{ill|Ikanopolis Plan|ja|イカノポリス計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikanopolis Plan|1=Ikanopolis Plan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131911088}}: A redevelopment concept for Hakodate after the closure of the {{ill|Seikan Ferry|ja|青函連絡船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikan Ferry|1=Seikan Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661111}}, spearheaded by the {{ill|Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja|北海道中小企業家同友会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Small and Medium Business Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hokkai Bon Odori|ja|北海盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkai Bon Odori|1=Hokkai Bon odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402409}}: A related festival dance held by neighborhood associations during the port festival.
* [http://www.hakodate-minatomatsuri.org/ Hakodate Port Festival Official Site] * [http://www.hakodatekids.net/web3d/ika_sw/ Let’s Dance Ika Odori - Hakodate Kids] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/deep/2011/07/post-175.html Let’s Dance Ika Odori at Hakodate Port Festival] – Hakodate Official Tourism Site * [https://wwwtb.mlit.go.jp/hokkaido/hakodate/cruise/event/event.html Event Info – Cruise Ship Farewell Team]
